japanese: ドアを開けてくれる？

cure_dolly_gloss: [gave-down-to-me:] — Can you open the door for me, please
answer_breakdown: くれる means 'give downward' — someone gives to me or my sphere. てくれる: [the subject] gives me the action of [開け]. The giver took the initiative. 'Downward' is not literally about height — it reflects the Japanese convention of placing oneself lower than others. If the action is given to me, it's くれる; if I give to others, it's あげる.

cure_dolly_gloss: [received the action of...] — Can you tell me what this is
answer_breakdown: もらう is a pull-receive verb — the receiver takes initiative. てもらう: the subject received/pulled the action of [教え] from the source (に-marked). Cure Dolly compares this directly to れる/られる: both are receptive (pull) verbs. Unlike くれる (where the giver takes initiative), here the receiver is in control.

cure_dolly_gloss: [received the action of...] — You were loved
answer_breakdown: もらう is a pull-receive verb — the receiver takes initiative. てもらう: the subject received/pulled the action of [可愛がっ] from the source (に-marked). Cure Dolly compares this directly to れる/られる: both are receptive (pull) verbs. Unlike くれる (where the giver takes initiative), here the receiver is in control.
reading: かわいがってもらってたんだね。
